title: Edit/Write to a main-checkout absolute path silently lands code in the wrong checkout
description: In a worktree session, tool calls that address files by the MAIN checkout path (C:/code/storytree/...) write there, not the worktree — invisible to the worktree's git status.
statement: When a session is anchored in a git worktree, an Edit/Write whose absolute path points at the MAIN checkout root (C:/code/storytree/apps/...) applies to the main checkout, not the worktree. The worktree's `git status` stays clean, so the misplacement is invisible until something else surfaces it — and the main checkout may be on a DIVERGENT branch, so the edits also land on the wrong baseline.
evidence: All ADR-0181 code (5 modified + 4 new files) was written to C:/code/storytree (on branch feat/corpus-librarian-credentials-sync) while the session ran in worktree optimistic-stonebraker-98be28. `git -C <worktree> status` showed only the ADR; `git -C C:/code/storytree status` showed the 5 M + 4 untracked desktop files. The main checkout's backend-entry.ts was also a divergent baseline (it had REMOVED broker-IPC code that origin/main carries), so the edits were against the wrong version. Recovery: git restore 5 files + rm 4 in the main checkout, ff-merge the 55-commits-behind worktree to origin/main, then re-apply every edit against the worktree's real files.
impact: Cost a full revert-and-redo cycle (~20 tool calls) plus diagnosis time to notice the split. Generalises the existing subagent-writes-to-main memory to FIRST-PERSON path slips: any worktree session that hardcodes the main-checkout root in a tool path hits it, and the clean worktree status hides it until late.
